goldpy.service

Quote normalization and selection logic.

This module is where the raw upstream feed becomes something easier to reason about. It handles pair validation, filtering, and the two quote selection strategies exposed by the CLI.

exception goldpy.service.QuoteSelectionError

Bases: GoldpyError

Raised when quote selection cannot produce a result.

class goldpy.service.CandidatePrice(platform, server, spread_profile, bid, ask, timestamp, spread, mid)

Bases: object

Internal flattened quote candidate used during selection.

The upstream payload nests prices under each source. For selection logic it is easier to work with one record per source/profile combination.

Parameters:

  • platform (str)

  • server (str)

  • spread_profile (str)

  • bid (object)

  • ask (object)

  • timestamp (object)

  • spread (object)

  • mid (object)

classmethod from_raw(quote, price)

Build a candidate from a raw quote and one spread-profile price.

Parameters:

  • quote (RawQuote) – Raw quote source metadata.

  • price (SpreadProfilePrice) – One spread-profile price entry from that source.

Returns:

A flattened CandidatePrice instance.

Return type:

CandidatePrice

goldpy.service.normalize_pair(pair)

Normalize and validate an instrument pair string.

The function accepts small variations in user input, trims whitespace, and uppercases both sides before validating the final BASE/QUOTE shape.

Parameters:

pair (str) – Pair supplied by the caller.

Returns:

Normalized pair string.

Raises:

QuoteSelectionError – If the pair does not look like BASE/QUOTE.

Return type:

str

goldpy.service.select_quote(pair, quotes, platform=None, spread_profile=None)

Select an aggregate quote from the available candidates.

The aggregate strategy chooses the best bid and best ask independently, then computes a midpoint from those two values. I like this mode because it answers the simple question of where the market edges currently sit.

Parameters:

  • pair (str) – Instrument pair being requested.

  • quotes (list[RawQuote]) – Raw quote sources returned by the client.

  • platform (str | None) – Optional platform filter.

  • spread_profile (str | None) – Optional spread profile filter.

Returns:

A normalized quote using the aggregate strategy.

Raises:

QuoteSelectionError – If filtering leaves no valid candidates.

Return type:

NormalizedQuote

goldpy.service.select_tightest_quote(pair, quotes, platform=None, spread_profile=None)

Select the tightest single quote from the available candidates.

This strategy keeps bid and ask together from one source/profile pair. When two candidates share the same spread, the higher midpoint wins.

Parameters:

  • pair (str) – Instrument pair being requested.

  • quotes (list[RawQuote]) – Raw quote sources returned by the client.

  • platform (str | None) – Optional platform filter.

  • spread_profile (str | None) – Optional spread profile filter.

Returns:

A normalized quote using the tightest strategy.

Raises:

QuoteSelectionError – If filtering leaves no valid candidates.

Return type:

NormalizedQuote

goldpy.service.available_platforms(quotes)

Return the sorted list of distinct platforms in the raw quotes.

Parameters:

quotes (list[RawQuote])

Return type:

list[str]

goldpy.service.available_spread_profiles(quotes)

Return the sorted list of distinct spread profiles in the raw quotes.

Parameters:

quotes (list[RawQuote])

Return type:

list[str]
